title: Notify User When User Type Changes
excerpt:

posts.author: M-a-x (Max Wolf)
posts.cookedHtml:
Feature request summary: add an admin setting to enable notifications to users when their user type changes.

Feature request details: the Customization > Features admin screen at …/admin/customization/features could have a new section (with two check boxes) that looks something like this:

User Type change notification
Generate a notification to a user when their User Type changes.
_ Notify user when User Type changes to Viewer
_ Notify user when User Type changes to Member

Use Cases
The use cases for our company are:

  1. When a Viewer requests to become a member, they should receive a confirmation that their User Type was changed. Currently in monday.com, when an admin changes a user from Viewer to Member, the user isn’t notified. This results in follow-up inquiries which could be avoided by a notification. (FYI, our volume is several requests per day, and we have thoroughly explored SCIM and other provisioning approaches.)
  2. We periodically change users from Member to Viewer if they haven’t accessed monday.com for X days. Currently in monday.com, when an admin changes a user from Member to Viewer , the user isn’t notified. The admin must manually notify the user. In our manual notification, we include a note that if the user wants to be able to edit and comment again, they should request to become a Member. If monday.com sent a notification automatically, that would save admin time.

Thank you!

posts.author: jayg (Jay)
posts.cookedHtml: it most definitely DOES notify users when the admin changes their status from member to viewer. Can this please be changed so accounts are not notified?

posts.author: YOGESHP (Yogesh Pokale)
posts.cookedHtml:
If you’re using a separate user directory, I’d strongly recommend managing access via SCIM rather than handling licenses directly in the application.

With SCIM, access is driven by directory group membership, which makes upgrades and downgrades straightforward and fully automated. You can also trigger and control notifications through your IdP workflows whenever a user’s license changes.

We use a similar model where accounts inactive for 120+ days are automatically downgraded to Viewer. If users need elevated access again, they submit a ticket and the workflow restores the appropriate license.

This approach keeps access governance clean, auditable, cost-efficient, and puts notification control in your hands.

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
Max Wolf:

Feature request summary: add an admin setting to enable notifications to users when their user type changes.

Agree — but for the opposite reason. I’d actually want a disable option.

Sometimes admins change a user type temporarily while troubleshooting permissions, testing workflows, or checking access issues. When monday immediately notifies the user, it can create confusion because the change wasn’t meant to be permanent.

It would be helpful if admins could:

• Disable the notification when needed
• Or at least have control over whether it’s sent

That way routine admin work or testing doesn’t accidentally trigger a bunch of “Why was my account changed?” messages.

If notifications do go out, it would also be great if admins could add context to the message explaining why the change happened.
